extra.message: Server-Side Template Injection via Jinja2. The 'name' query parameter from line 19 is interpolated into an f-string on line 38 to build HTML content, which is then passed to Template(content).render() on line 41. An attacker can inject Jinja2 template syntax (e.g., ?name={{7*6}} or {{config.__class__.__init__.__globals__['os'].popen('id').read()}}) to achieve Remote Code Execution.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF). The PUBLIC_IP_SERVICE_URL is loaded from an environment variable (config.py line 11) with no URL validation or allowlist. The requests.get() call on line 31 uses this untrusted URL. If an attacker can influence the environment variable (via container misconfiguration or .env file manipulation), they can force the server to make requests to arbitrary internal or external URLs, potentially accessing internal services or exfiltrating data.

Hardcoded credentials found in source code. SUPER_SECRET_NAME='John Ripper' and SUPER_SECRET_TOKEN='5u93R53Cr3tT0k3n' are hardcoded as string literals in lines 13-15. The FIXME comments indicate these should use os.getenv() instead. Hardcoded secrets in source code can be extracted by anyone with repository access and pose a critical security risk.
